content.cn_text: 导师:海伦和保罗，祝贺你们上个学期做得这么好。你们俩在第一年的护理项目中表现得无可挑剔。我想从你们两位那里得到一些反馈，以便更好地改进这个项目，并为我们未来的学生提供指导。我想从你开始，海伦。首先，这个项目的哪个方面让你印象深刻?
content.en_text: Tutor: Helen and Paul, congratulations to you both for doing so well the past semester. You two have exhibited an impeccable performance during your first year in the nursing program. I'd like to get some feedback from the two of you to better improve the program and to provide guidance for our prospective students. I'd like to start with you, Helen. So first of all,which aspect of the program impressed you?

content.cn_text: 海伦:嗯，说实话，当我报名参加这门课程的时候，我以为会有一群和我年龄相仿的同学。但当我第一次走进教室时，我对这里的多样性感到惊讶。大多数是20多岁，但也有30多岁甚至40多岁的人。事实证明，代际交流引发了激烈的辩论和新的思考，我认为这是我非常欣赏的节目的特别之处。
content.en_text: Helen: Well, to be honest, when I was enrolled into the course, I was expecting a group of classmates my age. But as I stepped into the classroom for the first time, I was surprised by the diversity. Most were in their twenties, but there were also those in their thirties or even forties. As it turns out, the intergenerational communication has sparked intense debate and new thinking, and I think that's something special about the program that I appreciate very much.

content.cn_text: 导师:保罗，你呢?你觉得这个节目怎么样?
content.en_text: Tutor: What about you, Paul? What do you think of the program?

content.cn_text: 保罗:对我来说，我们上学期进行的小组项目是这个项目的另一个主要特点。整个班级被分成8个不同的小组，研究8个视角案例。大多数时候，团队建设会议是以协作的方式进行的。
content.en_text: Paul: For me the group project we carried out last semester is another key feature of the program. The whole class was divided into 8 different groups working on 8 perspective cases. Team building sessions were conducted in a collaborative way most of the time.

content.cn_text: 我们小组由五名成员组成，研究急性胰腺炎。在这个过程中，我们把任务分成不同的部分，分配给每个成员。这样我们就能集中知识和技能来解决这个复杂的问题了。更重要的是，小组成员之间建立了更紧密的联系。因为这个项目，我们都成了好朋友。
content.en_text: Comprised of five members, our group studied acute pancreatitis. During the process, we broke the task into different parts and assigned them to each member. We were then able to tackle the complex problem by pooling our knowledge and skills. More importantly,stronger links were established between the group members. Because of the project,we've all become good friends.

content.cn_text: 导师:没错。毕业生表示，小组项目为他们进入团队合作日益成为常态的职场做好了准备。告诉我，保罗，你还喜欢这个项目的什么地方?
content.en_text: Tutor: That's true. According to graduates, group projects prepare them for the work world in which teamwork and collaboration are increasingly the norm. So tell me, Paul, what else do you like about the program?

content.cn_text: 保罗:毕业后我想成为一名在公立医院工作的注册护士。因此，在一个支持性的学习氛围中，实习提供了一个宝贵的临床实践机会。然而，我对wrr的数量感到惊讶。因为我认为这门课应该更注重实践导向的学习。
content.en_text: Paul: I want to be a registered nurse working in a public hospital after graduation. So the internship provided is a valuable opportunity for clinical practice in a supportive learning atmosphere. However, I was amazed by the amount of wrr.ten assignments since I thought the course should have focused more on practice-oriented learning.

content.cn_text: 海伦:嗯，我不同意你的观点，保罗。论文展示了你对课程的理解。对我来说，写论文是一个涉及批判性思维的过程，它挑战我更彻底地发展我的观点。我因此获得了不同的视角。
content.en_text: Helen: Well, I have to disagree with you, Paul. The essays demonstrate your understanding of the course. For me, writing essays is a process that involves critical thinking which challenges me to develop my points more thoroughly. I thus managed to gain a diversity of perspectives.

content.cn_text: 导师:该计划旨在提供包括医疗保健系统概念在内的核心概念的基本和高级理论知识，以及实习或临床实践经验，将课堂内容与实践环境联系起来。所以恐怕书面作业是不可避免的。今年，我们还增加了一个法律模块。你对此有何感想?
content.en_text: Tutor: The program is designed to deliver basic and advanced theoretical knowledge of core concepts including health care systems concepts, together with practicum or clinical practice experience to bridge the classroom content to the practice setting. So I'm afraid written work is unavoidable. Also this year, we've added a module of law. How do you feel about that?

content.cn_text: 海伦:起初我们觉得学习法律有点多余，而且太费时了。经过几次会议，我们意识到这在处理未来的医疗纠纷是必要的。
content.en_text: Helen: At first we felt that learning law is kind of redundant and too time-consuming. After a few sessions, we realised that it is necessary in dealing with future medical disputes.

content.cn_text: 导师:你对未来的学生有什么建议吗?
content.en_text: Tutor: Do you have any suggestions for prospective students?

content.cn_text: 保罗:最让我烦恼的是按时交论文。有一次我差点错过最后期限，因为同一周内有三篇论文要交。所以合理安排你的时间是至关重要的。
content.en_text: Paul: What bothers me most is handing in essays on time. I almost missed the deadline once because there were three essays due within the same week. So rationalising your time is critical.

content.cn_text: 海伦:嗯，这倒是真的。讲座提供了很多有用的信息。我的记忆力很差，所以我一直在做笔记，并定期回顾它们。令我惊讶的是，在学期结束时，我已经记住了关键的概念。
content.en_text: Helen: Well, that's true. The lectures deliver so much useful information. I have poor memory so I kept making notes and revisiting them on a regular basis. To my surprise, at the end of the semester, I have learnt the key concepts by heart.

content.cn_text: 导师:研究怎么样?我听说很有挑战性。你是怎样设法克服困难的?
content.en_text: Tutor: How was the research? I heard that it was quite challenging. How did you manage to overcome the difficulties?

content.cn_text: 保罗:没错。我们大多数人一开始都不知道如何进行这项研究。
content.en_text: Paul: That's true. The majority of us had no clue how to carry out the research at first.

content.cn_text: 幸运的是，当我在图书馆查找参考资料时，我向图书管理员寻求帮助。她教我如何寻找合适的资源和选择合适的研究方法。
content.en_text: Fortunately, when I was digging up reference materials at the library, I sought help from the librarian. She taught me about finding the appropriate resources and choosing the proper research methods.

content.cn_text: 导师:你看过网上论坛了吗?
content.en_text: Tutor: Have you checked out the online forum?

content.cn_text: 海伦:是的，定期访问论坛已经成为我的习惯了。从某种意义上说，它扩展了课堂学习。这是学生们发布他们遇到的学术问题并从教师那里得到支持的地方。
content.en_text: Helen: Yes, it has become a habit for me to visit the forum regularly. In a sense, it extends classroom learning. It is where the students post academic problems that they come across and get support from the faculty members.

content.cn_text: 保罗:我的一些同学在分班考试中表现不太好。我觉得背景阅读是必要的。
content.en_text: Paul: Some of my classmates didn't do so well during the placement tests. I feel that background reading is necessary.

content.cn_text: 导师:最后，你有什么话要对新生说吗?
content.en_text: Tutor: Lastly, do you have anything to say to the freshmen?

content.cn_text: 海伦:一开始我野心勃勃，想在成绩单上全得A。为了获得额外的学分，我做了大量的笔记，甚至在可选的作业上也很努力。在情绪崩溃之前，我给自己施加了很大压力。在咨询了我的导师之后，我发现设定现实的目标很重要。不要把自己逼得太紧。分清轻重缓急是明智的。
content.en_text: Helen: I was really ambitious at first, trying to get straight A's on my transcript. I made tons of notes and worked hard even on the optional assignments to get extra credit. I stressed myself out before having an emotional breakdown. After consulting my advisor, I found it important to set realistic goals. Don't push yourself too hard. It is wise to sort out your priorities.

content.cn_text: 导师:感谢您今天来到这里，并对这个项目提供了宝贵的反馈。祝你暑假愉快。
content.en_text: Tutor: Thank you for coming here today and providing valuable feedback on the program. Have a great summer break.

groups.desc:
Questions 21-24
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

items.title: How old are the students on the nursing program?

options.title: C
options.content: They belong to different age groups

items.analysis: 文中明确指出学生的年龄范围包括二十几岁、三十几岁和四十几岁，这表明学生属于不同的年龄组。Helen提到她对班级的年龄多样性感到惊讶，这进一步强调了学生年龄的多样性。因此，选项C"They belong to different age groups"最准确地描述了护理课程学生的年龄情况。

items.title: What do the speakers say about the group project?

options.title: A
options.content: It helps to improve relationship among different classmates.

options.title: B
options.content: It helps to develop problem solving skills.

items.analysis: 文中Paul提到小组项目是课程的一个关键特征，并强调通过这个项目，小组成员之间建立了更强的联系，最终都成为了好朋友。这直接体现了小组项目改善了同学之间的关系，符合选项A的描述。虽然Paul也提到了问题解决和学习环境的方面，但他最后强调的是人际关系的改善，这是本题的核心点。

items.title: Which part of the program surprised Paul?

options.title: A
options.content: There's a number of essays to write.

options.title: B
options.content: There's a lot of practical work.

items.analysis: Paul 在谈到课程时表达了惊讶之情，特别是对写作作业的数量感到意外。他使用了"amazed"一词，这与题目中的"surprised"同义。Paul原本期望课程更侧重于实践，但实际上有大量的写作作业，这与他的预期不符，因此让他感到惊讶。这直接对应了选项A中提到的大量essays（写作作业）。

items.title: What do they feel about learning law?

options.title: C
options.content: It takes up too much time.

items.analysis: 文中提到学生最初认为学习法律是多余且耗时的，但是经过几节课后，他们意识到学习法律对于处理未来的医疗纠纷是必要的。这表明他们最终认识到法律学习的重要性，因此正确答案是A选项"It is essential training"。"necessary"（必要的）与"essential"（必不可少的）在此语境中是同义词，都表示法律学习的重要性。

groups.desc:
Questions 25-30
What are the suggestions offered by the speakers?
Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-H, next to
Questions 25-30.

list.title: C
list.content: get help from school supporting staff

items.title: Essays
items.analysis: 文中Paul提到最困扰他的是按时提交论文，并举例说明同一周内有多篇论文需要提交导致他差点错过截止日期。他强调"rationalising your time is critical"（合理安排时间至关重要），这直接对应了选项E "manage time properly"（合理管理时间）。这表明在处理论文任务时，时间管理是最关键的建议。

items.title: Lectures
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是G（review the notes regularly），因为Helen在谈到lectures时提到她的学习方法是"kept making notes and revisiting them on a regular basis"。这正是"定期复习笔记"的意思。她还强调这种方法的效果很好，说到学期结束时她已经把关键概念都记住了。这个建议直接针对如何应对lectures，与题干要求完全吻合。

items.title: Research
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C（get help from school supporting staff），因为原文中Paul明确提到他在进行研究时遇到困难，并向图书管理员（属于学校支持人员）寻求帮助。图书管理员教导他如何找到合适的资源和选择适当的研究方法，这正是学校支持人员提供帮助的具体体现。此外，"sought help"（寻求帮助）直接对应选项C中的"get help"，进一步确认了这个选项的正确性。

items.title: Online forum
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A（get feedback from teaching staff），因为原文中提到学生在论坛上可以"get support from the faculty members"。虽然原文没有直接使用"feedback"这个词，但在学术环境中，从教职员那里获得支持实际上就是获得反馈。这需要考生理解学术语境并进行一定的推理。此外，"faculty members"与选项中的"teaching staff"是同义词，进一步确认了A选项的正确性。

items.title: Placement tests
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B（do more reading）。Paul提到一些同学在placement tests中表现不佳，然后他建议"background reading is necessary"。这里的"background reading"直接对应选项B中的"do more reading"。这个建议是针对placement tests的，因此是最合适的答案。其他选项虽然也可能是有用的建议，但它们并不是特别针对placement tests的。

items.title: Freshmen
items.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是H（don't set unrealistic goals），因为在原文中Helen分享了她自己的经历，她最初设定了不切实际的目标（想全部得A），结果导致了情绪崩溃。后来她意识到设定现实的目标的重要性，并建议不要给自己太大压力。这个建议直接针对新生（freshmen），符合题目要求，同时也是对Helen个人经历的总结和提炼。
